title: Podman Machine does not work out of the box on a fresh install of Fedora Workstation 36
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm new to and learning about _Podman Machine_ and got an error which I haven't further researched much yet, but can't find much interesting about when searching for it online, so I thought I would let you know about it here. Presumably you meant for this to "just work"? Perhaps the experience could be improved with a better error message or even just some doc update somewhere. I'm happy to help contribute it, once I understand the root cause better.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Fresh install of Fedora Workstation 36, all packages up to date, no additional new packages installed yet
1. `podman machine init`
1. `podman machine start`

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: unable to start host networking: "could not find \"gvproxy\" in one of [/usr/local/libexec/podman /usr/local/lib/podman /usr/libexec/podman /usr/lib/podman].  To resolve this error, set the helper_binaries_dir key in the `[engine]` section of containers.conf to the directory containing your helper binaries."`

**Describe the results you expected:**

No error, working podman machine.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.3
Built:        Wed Jun 15 16:31:58 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

comments.body:
Oh OK I see, I just poked around this a little bit... so this `gvproxy` thing is from https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock (from https://gvisor.dev), and it's missing on a fresh install of Fedora Workstation 36. 

I was able to get it to work after:

    sudo dnf install -y podman-gvproxy

Perhaps the "first user experience" could be improved here. Perhaps you would like to consider making `podman-gvproxy` a dependency of the `podman` package, if there is no reason not to?

I am not sure if it should be a recommends or not, definitely not a requires.  I agree, podman machine should throw an error or warning when it is not installed, and clearly as the user to install it.

@ashley-cui PTAL

> I am not sure if it should be a recommends or not, definitely not a requires. I agree, podman machine should throw an error or warning when it is not installed, and clearly as the user to install it.

@rhatdan currently `podman-plugins` does have `Recommends: podman-gvproxy` but `podman` itself has no dependency on podman-plugins or podman-gvproxy. If the main podman package provides the `podman machine` command, then I think podman should have a hard requirement on podman-gvproxy. @mheon @vrothberg wdyt?
IMHO There is no reason to install it by default. I mean how many people want to run podman machine on fedora?
Can we make podman recommends podman-plugins?
podman-plugins only contains the dnsname cni plugin, we do not need/want this on netavark/aardvark systems.
Ok let's just add recommends gvproxy to podman.

> Ok let's just add recommends gvproxy to podman.

added to rawhide, will add to f36 whenever we cut a new release.

I'm gonna close this issue as the fix will land in fedora soon and it's not really an upstream issue.

Reopen if anything..
